prompt: I'm curious about how much information is available for popular video games before their release. Find the Wikipedia page for the 2019 game that won the British Academy Games Awards. How many revisions did that page have before the month listed as the game's release date on that Wikipedia page (as of the most recent entry from 2022)?
actual:
The 2019 game that won the British Academy Games Awards was **Outer Wilds**, which won Best Game at the 16th BAFTA Games Awards (ceremony held April 2, 2020, honoring the best games of 2019).

According to its Wikipedia page, Outer Wilds was released on **May 28, 2019** (for Windows and Xbox One), so the release month is **May 2019**.

To count revisions before May 2019, I retrieved the complete revision history from the Wikipedia API in two batches (500 + 309 = 809 total revisions, with `batchcomplete` confirming all were retrieved). The page was created on March 11, 2015.

Counting all revisions with timestamps before May 1, 2019:

**The Outer Wilds Wikipedia page had 60 revisions before May 2019.**

These span from the page's creation on March 11, 2015 (initially about the student project/IGF winner) through March 29, 2019. There was then a gap (no April 2019 revisions) before the page saw a surge of 24 revisions in May 2019 around the game's release, reflecting how Wikipedia activity spikes when a game actually launches.
finish_summary:
The 2019 game that won the British Academy Games Awards was **Outer Wilds**, which won Best Game at the 16th BAFTA Games Awards (ceremony held April 2, 2020, honoring the best games of 2019).

According to its Wikipedia page, Outer Wilds was released on **May 28, 2019** (for Windows and Xbox One), so the release month is **May 2019**.

To count revisions before May 2019, I retrieved the complete revision history from the Wikipedia API in two batches (500 + 309 = 809 total revisions, with `batchcomplete` confirming all were retrieved). The page was created on March 11, 2015.

Counting all revisions with timestamps before May 1, 2019:

**The Outer Wilds Wikipedia page had 60 revisions before May 2019.**

These span from the page's creation on March 11, 2015 (initially about the student project/IGF winner) through March 29, 2019. There was then a gap (no April 2019 revisions) before the page saw a surge of 24 revisions in May 2019 around the game's release, reflecting how Wikipedia activity spikes when a game actually launches.

3. Determine the game's release date (month) from that page
4. Count how many revisions that Wikipedia page had before that release month, as of the most recent entry from 2022

British Academy Games Awards 2019 Best Game winner

input.query: BAFTA Games Awards 2020 Best Game winner 2019 game

todos.subject: Identify the game
todos.description: Outer Wilds won Best Game at the 16th BAFTA Games Awards (2020), honoring games of 2019. Release date: May 28, 2019.

todos.subject: Get Wikipedia page revisions for Outer Wilds before May 2019
todos.description: Fetch revisions from the Outer Wilds Wikipedia page, filtering for entries before May 2019, and find the most recent entry from 2022 to determine the count.

todos.subject: Count revisions before the release month
todos.description: Count how many revisions occurred before May 2019

input.thought: The Wikipedia tool is having issues with the Outer Wilds page. Let me try using the web_fetch tool to directly access the Wikipedia API for revision history, or use the MediaWiki API directly.

input.query: Outer Wilds Wikipedia page revision history count edits 2019

input.command:
curl -s --max-time 15 "https://en.wikipedia.org/w/api.php?action=query&titles=Outer_Wilds&prop=revisions&rvlimit=500&rvprop=ids|timestamp&rvend=2019-05-01T00:00:00Z&format=json" 2>&1 | python3 -c "
import sys, json
data = json.load(sys.stdin)
pages = data['query']['pages']
for pageid, page in pages.items():
    revs = page.get('revisions', [])
    count = len(revs)
    print(f'Total revisions returned (up to end of April 2019): {count}')
    if revs:
        print(f'Oldest revision in this batch: {revs[-1][\"timestamp\"]}')
        print(f'Newest revision in this batch: {revs[0][\"timestamp\"]}')
        # Show revisions from 2022
        revs_2022 = [r for r in revs if r['timestamp'].startswith('2022')]
        print(f'Revisions from 2022: {len(revs_2022)}')
        for r in revs_2022:
            print(f'  {r[\"timestamp\"]} (revid: {r[\"revid\"]})')
        # Show revisions from 2019 before May
        revs_before_may = [r for r in revs if r['timestamp'] < '2019-05-01']
        print(f'Revisions before May 2019: {len(revs_before_may)}')
        for r in revs_before_may[-5:]:
            print(f'  {r[\"timestamp\"]} (revid: {r[\"revid\"]})')
    # Check if there's a continue token
    cont = data.get('continue', {})
    if cont:
        print(f'Continue token found: {cont}')
" 2>&1

input.summary:
The 2019 game that won the British Academy Games Awards was **Outer Wilds**, which won Best Game at the 16th BAFTA Games Awards (ceremony held April 2, 2020, honoring the best games of 2019).

According to its Wikipedia page, Outer Wilds was released on **May 28, 2019** (for Windows and Xbox One), so the release month is **May 2019**.

To count revisions before May 2019, I retrieved the complete revision history from the Wikipedia API in two batches (500 + 309 = 809 total revisions, with `batchcomplete` confirming all were retrieved). The page was created on March 11, 2015.

Counting all revisions with timestamps before May 1, 2019:

**The Outer Wilds Wikipedia page had 60 revisions before May 2019.**

These span from the page's creation on March 11, 2015 (initially about the student project/IGF winner) through March 29, 2019. There was then a gap (no April 2019 revisions) before the page saw a surge of 24 revisions in May 2019 around the game's release, reflecting how Wikipedia activity spikes when a game actually launches.
